samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. The data_en input is synchronous to clk_a and the data_in input is constant during the period when data_en is high. So, if data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b cycles, then data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. The clock clk_a is responsible for data_in and data_en. The clock clk_b is responsible for the 2 D flip-flops.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="enum_0">
            <enum_value id="off">off</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="on">on</enum_value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="data_reg" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Data register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_data_reg" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Enable data register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_one" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Enable control register one.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_two" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Enable control register two.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <p1>Register data_reg:</p1>
        <p2>Update data_reg on the positive edge of clk_a or the negative edge of arstn.</p2>
        <p3>If arstn is low, data_reg is 0.</p3>
        <p4>Otherwise, data_reg is data_in.</p4>
        <p5>Register en_data_reg:</p5>
        <p6>Update en_data_reg on the positive edge of clk_a or the negative edge of arstn.</p6>
        <p7>If arstn is low, en_data_reg is 0.</p7>
        <p8>Otherwise, en_data_reg is data_en.</p8>
        <p9>Register en_clap_one:</p9>
        <p10>Update en_clap_one on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn.</p10>
        <p11>If brstn is low, en_clap_one is 0.</p11>
        <p12>Otherwise, en_clap_one is en_data_reg.</p12>
        <p13>Register en_clap_two:</p13>
        <p14>Update en_clap_two on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn.</p14>
        <p15>If brstn is low, en_clap_two is 0.</p15>
        <p16>Otherwise, en_clap_two is en_clap_one.</p16>
        <p17>Output dataout:</p17>
        <p18>Assign dataout on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn.</p18>
        <p19>If brstn is low, dataout is 0.</p19>
        <p20>Otherwise, if en_clap_two is on, dataout is data_reg.</p20>
        <p21>Otherwise, dataout is dataout.</p21>
    </implementation>
</module>
